title: Agentic AI for Financial Risk Operations
abstract: We present a retrieval-grounded agent architecture for financial risk operations. The approach combines tool use, planning, and document retrieval to support analysts. Experiments on internal-style compliance and risk-review tasks show improved accuracy over single-shot baselines. Limitations include dataset scarcity and governance overhead. Future work includes stronger benchmark coverage and human review integration.

title: Benchmarking Tool-Using LLM Agents in Regulated Analytics
abstract: This paper studies how large language model agents perform in regulated analytics environments. We introduce a benchmark for tool use, policy retrieval, and multi-step reasoning. Results show that grounded agent pipelines outperform plain prompting while still failing on long-horizon compliance cases. Future work should expand evaluation datasets and oversight mechanisms.

title: Retrieval-Augmented Review Agents for Financial Document Intelligence
abstract: We propose review agents that synthesize long financial documents using retrieval-augmented generation. The method improves factual consistency on earnings-call and policy-review datasets. However, benchmark coverage is narrow and real deployment requires robust reviewer controls.

title: Evaluation Patterns for Financial LLM Agents
abstract: This study examines evaluation design for financial language model agents. We compare expert review, benchmark scoring, and operational error analysis. Findings show that benchmark-only evaluation misses domain failures. The paper highlights future work in longitudinal testing and governance-aware metrics.
